text: Stores product category definitions and metadata, serving as a lookup table for classifying products in the inventory system.
Associative table linking customer entities to demographic profiles, facilitating many-to-many relationships between customers and customer types.

Stores descriptive characteristics and demographic profiles associated with customer types. This table defines the categories of customers and their corresponding descriptions, serving as a lookup table for customer classification.
A table storing detailed information about customers, including contact details, demographics, and shipping addresses.
A table containing detailed information about the company's employees, including personal details, contact information, job titles, and organizational hierarchy.
A junction table linking employees to their assigned sales territories, enabling many-to-many relationships between staff and geographic regions.

Stores individual line items for each order, linking products to orders with specific pricing, quantities, and discounts. This table captures the details of items purchased in a single order transaction.
Stores details about customer orders including dates, shipping information, assigned employees, and shipping carriers.
A master table storing details for all products in the inventory, linking to categories and suppliers to manage product information, pricing, and stock levels.
Stores distinct geographical region definitions used to categorize territories.
Stores information about shipping companies used for delivering orders.
Stores detailed information about product suppliers, including company details, contact persons, and geographical data.
Contains geographic territory definitions including descriptions and their associated regions for sales or distribution tracking.
Unique integer identifier for each product category, acting as the primary key for the Categories table.
Textual name representing the category group, such as Seafood, Dairy Products, or Beverages.
Detailed text explaining the contents or characteristics of the product category.
Binary large object storing the visual representation or icon associated with a specific product category.
Unique identifier referencing a specific customer record from the Customers table.
Unique identifier referencing a specific demographic type from the CustomerDemographics table.

Unique identifier for a specific customer type or demographic category. Acts as the primary key for the CustomerDemographics table and is referenced by the CustomerCustomerDemo table to link customers to their demographic groups.
Descriptive text providing details about the specific customer type or demographic segment. This field offers context on the characteristics or attributes defining the customer category.
A unique identifier for each customer, serving as the primary key.
The name of the customer's business or organization.
The name of the primary contact person for the customer.
The job title or role of the contact person within the company.
The street address of the customer's location.
The city where the customer is located.
The region or state/province where the customer is located.
The postal or zip code associated with the customer's address.
The country where the customer is located.
The telephone number of the customer.
The fax number of the customer.
Unique identifier for each employee, serving as the primary key for the Employees table and referenced by other tables for relationships.
The surname or family name of the employee.
The given name or first name of the employee.
The job title or position held by the employee within the organization.
The honorific or courtesy title used when addressing the employee (e.g., Mr., Ms., Mrs., Dr.).
The date of birth of the employee.
The date when the employee was hired by the company.
The home street address of the employee.
The city where the employee resides.
The geographical region or state where the employee is located.
The postal or zip code associated with the employee's home address.
The country where the employee resides.
The home telephone number of the employee.
The internal telephone extension number for the employee.
A binary large object (BLOB) containing the employee's photograph.
Additional text notes or biographical details about the employee.
The EmployeeID of the manager to whom this employee reports, indicating the organizational reporting line.
A text string containing the URL or file path to the employee's photograph.
Unique identifier for the employee assigned to a specific territory.
Unique identifier for the sales territory or geographic region associated with an employee.
Unique identifier for the order that this line item belongs to. Links the detail record back to the main Orders table.
Unique identifier for the product included in this order detail. Links the detail record back to the Products table.
The price of a single unit of the product at the time of the order. This price is recorded statically to preserve historical accuracy of the transaction.
The number of units of the specific product ordered in this line item.
The discount percentage applied to this specific line item. A value of 0 indicates no discount, while higher values indicate greater discounts up to the maximum allowed.
The unique identifier for each sales order.
The unique identifier linking the order to a specific customer.
The unique identifier linking the order to the employee who processed it.
The date and time when the order was placed.
The date by which the customer expects the order to be delivered.
The date and time when the order was actually shipped.
The identifier for the shipping carrier or company used to deliver the order.
The cost of freight or shipping charged for the order.
The name of the customer or company to whom the order is being shipped.
The street address where the order is to be delivered.
The city where the order is to be delivered.
The region, state, or province where the order is to be delivered.
The postal or zip code for the shipping destination.
The country where the order is to be delivered.
The unique identifier for each product in the system.
The name or title of the product.
The identifier linking the product to its supplier in the Suppliers table.
The identifier linking the product to its category in the Categories table.
The packaging unit quantity or size description for the product.
The selling price for a single unit of the product.
The current number of units of the product available in inventory.
The number of units of the product that are currently ordered from the supplier but not yet received.
The minimum quantity of the product in stock that triggers a reorder notification.
A flag indicating whether the product is discontinued (active or inactive status).
Unique integer identifier for each geographical region.
Textual name or label describing the geographical region.
Unique identifier for each shipping company.
Name of the shipping company.
Contact phone number for the shipping company.
The unique identifier for each supplier.
The legal or trading name of the supplier company.
The name of the primary contact person at the supplier.
The job title or role of the contact person within the supplier company.
The street address of the supplier's location.
The city where the supplier is located.
The region or state/province of the supplier's location.
The postal or zip code for the supplier's address.
The country where the supplier is based.
The primary telephone number for the supplier.
The fax number associated with the supplier's business contact.
The website URL or homepage link for the supplier.
Unique identifier for each sales territory, typically formatted as a postal code.
Descriptive name or label for the territory, often representing a city or specific area.
Foreign key linking the territory to its broader geographic region.
